ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_code" TEXT /* Unique code for each country (e.g., PY for Paraguay) */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "continent" TEXT /* Continent where the country is located */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO code of the country (e.g., PY for Paraguay) */,
  "capital" TEXT /* Capital city of the country */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_code")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_pages" (
  "page_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data page */,
  "country_code" TEXT /* Reference to the country the page belongs to */,
  "page_number" INTEGER /* Page number of the dataset */,
  "page_size" TEXT /* Size of the page (e.g., A4, Letter) */,
  "data_format" TEXT /* Format of the data (e.g., CSV, JSON) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("page_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_pages_country_code FOREIGN KEY ("country_code") REFERENCES countries ("country_code")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_ranges" (
  "range_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each geographic range */,
  "page_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data page the range belongs to */,
  "range_value" TEXT /* Value representing the geographic range */,
  "range_type" TEXT /* Type of the range (e.g., latitude, longitude) */,
  "range_description" TEXT /* Description of the range */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("range_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_ranges_page_id FOREIGN KEY ("page_id") REFERENCES data_pages ("page_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each type of geographic data */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the data type (e.g., geographic, proper) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data type */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the data type (e.g., physical, cultural) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "providers" (
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each provider */,
  "provider_name" TEXT /* Name of the provider offering the geographic data */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the provider */,
  "organization_type" TEXT /* Type of organization (e.g., government, private) */,
  "reputation_score" REAL /* Reputation score of the provider (e.g., 1-5) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("provider_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_records" (
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data record */,
  "page_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data page the record relates to */,
  "range_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the geographic range of the record */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the type of data */,
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the provider of the data */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the data (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "update_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of updates to the data (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_records_page_id FOREIGN KEY ("page_id") REFERENCES data_pages ("page_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_records_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("type_id") REFERENCES data_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_records_range_id FOREIGN KEY ("range_id") REFERENCES data_ranges ("range_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_records_provider_id FOREIGN KEY ("provider_id") REFERENCES providers ("provider_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., admin, user) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role's responsibilities */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the role (e.g., full, read-only) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Role of the user defined in the user_roles table */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data record being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user accessing the record */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date of the access event */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., read, write) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_record_id FOREIGN KEY ("record_id") REFERENCES data_records ("record_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_analytics" (
  "analytics_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytics entry */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data record being analyzed */,
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the provider of the data */,
  "data_distribution" TEXT /* Distribution of the data (e.g., normal, skewed) */,
  "data_coverage" REAL /* Coverage of the data (e.g., percentage of area covered) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytics_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_analytics_provider_id FOREIGN KEY ("provider_id") REFERENCES providers ("provider_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_analytics_record_id FOREIGN KEY ("record_id") REFERENCES data_records ("record_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_visualizations" (
  "visualization_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each visualization entry */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data record being visualized */,
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the provider of the data */,
  "visualization_type" TEXT /* Type of visualization (e.g., map, chart) */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date of the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("visualization_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_visualizations_record_id FOREIGN KEY ("record_id") REFERENCES data_records ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_visualizations_provider_id FOREIGN KEY ("provider_id") REFERENCES providers ("provider_id")
)
